prose:
Frontier-orbital single-point calculation on TEMPO (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxyl) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fitted) in PySCF. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 2 (open-shell doublet, UKS). Geometry: user-supplied .xyz at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz, used as-is (single point, no re-optimization). SCF converged in 12 cycles. No warnings reported. Integrity: trustworthy = true (all checks passed).

Results:
- HOMO energy: −6.3375439 eV (index 86, occupation 1.0)
- LUMO energy: −2.4651235 eV (index 87, occupation 0.0)
- HOMO–LUMO gap: 3.8724203 eV (headline value)
- Total energy: −483.88366 Hartree (−13,167.145 eV)

Koopmans-theorem descriptors:
- Vertical IP: 6.3375439 eV
- Vertical EA: 2.4651235 eV
- Electronegativity: 4.4013337 eV
- Chemical hardness: 1.9362102 eV
- Chemical softness: 0.51647286 eV⁻¹
- Electrophilicity index: 5.0024886 eV

These are Koopmans-theorem orbital energies (not ΔSCF or explicitly correlated). The total energy is the bare electronic energy and is not comparable across methods or basis sets.
